[828]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 武林徐氏 - Refers to the Xu family of Wulin (Hangzhou), indicating a prominent family in Zhejiang.
  • 浙西望族 - Means 'a distinguished family in western Zhejiang,' highlighting their social status.
  • 故第 - Refers to the old mansion or ancestral home.
  • 姚园寺巷 - A specific location in Hangzhou, likely a street or alley.
  • 乱后虽毁于兵火 - Means 'though destroyed by war after the chaos,' indicating the impact of war on the family's property.
  • 门楣及听事无恙 - Means 'the door lintel and the main hall remained intact,' emphasizing what survived the destruction.
  • 至今犹存 - Means 'still exist today,' indicating the longevity of these inscriptions.
  • 余门下士花农庶常琪 - Refers to a student named Huā Nóng (花农) who was a scholar-official (shùcháng, 庶常).
  • 乃文穆公来孙也 - Means 'he is the descendant of Wen Mu Gong (文穆公),' indicating his lineage.
  • 稍加葺治而居之 - Means 'he slightly repaired and lived in it,' describing the restoration of the house.
  • 辄兴故家乔木之思 - Means 'I always feel a sense of nostalgia for the old family and its tall trees,' expressing the translator's sentiment.
  • 偶阅定远方莲舫太守《蔗余偶笔》 - Means 'I happened to read in the *Zheyu Oubi* (蔗余偶笔) by the governor of Dingzhou, Dìng Fāng (定方), known as Lianfang (莲舫),' indicating the source of the information.
  • 载其家文敬公轶事云 - Means 'it records some anecdotes about Wen Jing Gong (文敬公) of the family,' introducing the anecdotes.
  • 文敬之封翁居钱唐江边 - Means 'Wen Jing's father-in-law lived by the Qiantang River,' providing context for the setting.
  • 每子午潮退 - Means 'every time the high tide receded,' indicating a regular event.
  • 将沿岸数十里水族 - Means 'he would sweep the aquatic creatures along the coast for dozens of miles,' describing a specific action.
  • 亲扫入江 - Means 'he personally swept them into the river,' emphasizing the father-in-law's involvement.
  • 自少至壮无间 - Means 'from youth to adulthood without interruption,' indicating the duration of the practice.
  • 一夜潮极大 - Means 'one night the tide was extremely high,' setting up a dramatic event.
  • 巡江武官见火光一团 - Means 'a military officer patrolling the river saw a ball of fire,' describing the sighting.
  • 涌入徐氏 - Means 'rush into the Xu family's residence,' indicating the direction of the fire.
  • 叩门以告 - Means 'knock on the door to report,' describing the action taken.
  • 适文敬公生 - Means 'just as Wen Jing Gong was born,' indicating a coincidence.
  • 因以潮名 - Means 'so he named him after the tide,' explaining the naming.
  • 及观平江李次青廉访《国朝先正事略》 - Means 'and when I read the *Guochao Xianzheng Shilüe* (国朝先正事略) by Li Ciqing (李次青), a censor from Pingjiang,' indicating another source.
  • 钱唐徐文敬公 - Refers to Wen Jing Gong (文敬公) of the Xu family from Qiantang (钱唐).
  • 先世业渔 - Means 'his ancestors were fishermen,' providing background.
  • 公生日 - Means 'the day he was born,' indicating the specific event.
  • 江潮大上 - Means 'the river tide rose greatly,' describing the natural phenomenon.
  • 一舟为风漂没 - Means 'a boat was swept away by the wind,' indicating a tragic event.
  • 封翁急拯之得免 - Means 'the father-in-law hurriedly rescued him and saved him,' describing the rescue.
  • 僦舟者为闽省孝廉 - Means 'the boat owner was a scholar from Fujian,' providing additional context.
  • 入都应试者也 - Means 'who came to the capital to take the exams,' indicating the purpose of the trip.
  • 翁延至家 - Means 'the father-in-law invited him home,' describing the hospitality.
  • 致鸡黍之敬 - Means 'to show respect by serving him chicken and millet,' indicating the gesture of respect.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武林 - Refers to Hangzhou, historically known as Wulin.
  • 浙西 - Refers to western Zhejiang Province.
  • 圣祖 - Refers to the Kangxi Emperor (1654-1722), the fourth emperor of the Qing dynasty.
  • 高宗 - Refers to the Qianlong Emperor (1711-1799), the sixth emperor of the Qing dynasty.
  • 文穆公 - Refers to a noble title, likely a posthumous name given to a member of the Xu family.
  • 文敬公 - Another noble title, likely referring to a different member of the Xu family.
  • 钱唐 - Refers to Qiantang, an ancient name for Hangzhou.
  • 平江 - Refers to Suzhou, another city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 国朝先正事略 - A historical work that records the lives of notable figures from the Qing dynasty.
  • 孝廉 - A title given to scholars who passed the provincial exams and were recommended for the imperial exams.
  • 鸡黍 - A traditional Chinese dish of chicken and millet, often served to show hospitality.

Proper Nouns

  • 武林徐氏 - The Xu family of Wulin (Hangzhou).
  • 浙西 - Western Zhejiang.
  • 姚园寺巷 - A specific location in Hangzhou.
  • 圣祖 - The Kangxi Emperor.
  • 高宗 - The Qianlong Emperor.
  • 文穆公 - A noble title.
  • 文敬公 - Another noble title.
  • 钱唐 - Qiantang (ancient name for Hangzhou).
  • 平江 - Suzhou.
  • 国朝先正事略 - A historical work.
  • 孝廉 - A title for scholars.
  • 鸡黍 - A traditional dish.
  • 定远方莲舫太守 - Dìng Fāng (定方), known as Lianfang (莲舫), the governor of Dingzhou.
  • 李次青廉访 - Li Ciqing (李次青), a censor from Pingjiang.

[830]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 赘婿 - A man who marries into his wife's family and lives with her family.
  • 不兼 - An archaic character meaning 'to hate' or 'to dislike.'
  • 高车 - A high carriage, often used metaphorically to denote a person of high status.
  • 白屋 - A humble dwelling, often used to refer to a poor family.
  • 青云之士 - A person of high rank or noble status.
  • 高门鼎族 - A family of high social status and prestige.
  • 白屋之中大有青云之士 - A phrase meaning that even among poor families, there can be people of high rank or noble status.
  • 勿以高门鼎族而蔑视寒微 - A phrase meaning do not look down on those from poor families just because you come from a prestigious family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 文敬公 - A title given to a respected official, possibly referring to a historical figure.
  • 殳氏 - A surname, likely referring to a family.
  • 江氏 - A surname, possibly referring to a family.
  • 韦家 - A surname, possibly referring to a family.
  • 槟榔之诮 - A reference to a specific incident or anecdote involving the Jiang family and a reprimand.
  • 鹳雀之称 - A reference to a specific incident or anecdote involving the Wei family and a derogatory nickname.
  • 白屋之中大有青云之士 - A common saying in Chinese literature, emphasizing that even among the poor, there can be people of high status.
  • 勿以高门鼎族而蔑视寒微 - A moral lesson often found in Chinese literature, advising against looking down on the less fortunate.

Proper Nouns

  • 文敬公 - A title given to a respected official.
  • 殳氏 - A surname, likely referring to a family.
  • 江氏 - A surname, possibly referring to a family.
  • 韦家 - A surname, possibly referring to a family.
  • 槟榔 - A plant and its fruit, often used in traditional Chinese medicine and as a snack.
  • 鹳雀 - A type of bird, often associated with good luck in Chinese culture.

[831]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旌德观 - A temple dedicated to the worship of a deity, specifically one associated with the suppression of epidemics.
  • 疫神曰温元帅 - The god of epidemics is called Wen Yuan Shuai.
  • 盖亦逐疫之意 - Presumably an attempt to drive away epidemics.
  • 实与吏部无涉 - This is not related to the Ministry of Personnel.
  • 巫觋之见 - Such beliefs of shamans are truly laughable.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杭州姚园寺巷 - A specific location in Hangzhou, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 温元帅 - A deity associated with the suppression of epidemics, often revered in Chinese folk religion.
  • 吴自牧《梦粱录》 - *Menglianglu* (Dreams of Splendor) is a historical text from the Yuan dynasty that documents the customs and culture of the Song dynasty.
  • 宋世 - The Song dynasty (960-1279), a period of significant cultural and technological development in China.
  • 徐文敬公 - Xu Wenjing, a historical figure who served as the Grand Master of Ceremonies, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 天官 - The title of the Grand Master of Ceremonies, responsible for overseeing religious and ceremonial matters.
  • 礼部祠祭司 - The Sacrificial Affairs Office of the Ministry of Rites, responsible for managing religious and ceremonial activities.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel, responsible for civil service appointments and administration.
  • 巫觋 - Shamans or sorcerers, often associated with folk religious practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 杭州 - Hangzhou, a major city in eastern China.
  • 姚园寺巷 - A specific street in Hangzhou.
  • 旌德观 - The temple dedicated to the deity of epidemics.
  • 温元帅 - The deity of epidemics.
  • 吴自牧 - Wu Zimu, the author of *Menglianglu*.
  • 梦粱录 - *Menglianglu* (Dreams of Splendor).
  • 宋世 - The Song dynasty.
  • 徐文敬公 - Xu Wenjing, a historical figure.
  • 天官 - The Grand Master of Ceremonies.
  • 礼部祠祭司 - The Sacrificial Affairs Office of the Ministry of Rites.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel.
  • 巫觋 - Shamans or sorcerers.

[832]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 入门，有女子六七人执帚扫花 - Literally 'entering the door, there are six or seven women holding brooms sweeping flowers.' Sets a scene with a specific number of women performing a task.
  • 视徐而笑，不出一语 - Means 'looking at Xu and smiling, but not speaking a word,' indicating a silent, enigmatic reaction.
  • 天下今宵共明月，人间何处有仙山 - A poetic couplet suggesting a shared experience of the moonlight and questioning the existence of mythical places.
  • 堂之左右，列书橱八九 - Means 'on both sides of the hall, there are eight or nine bookshelves,' indicating a well-organized library.
  • 缥囊缃帙，充刃其中 - Refers to 'books with colorful bindings and covers, filling the shelves,' emphasizing the richness of the collection.
  • 角巾野服者数辈，挟册讽诵 - Means 'several people wearing headbands and rustic clothing, holding books and reciting,' depicting a scholarly scene.
  • 樵者即取一卷，授徐视之 - Means 'the woodcutter immediately took a scroll, gave it to Xu to look at,' indicating a significant gesture.
  • 惊而寤，不解所谓 - Means 'Xu was startled awake, not understanding what it meant,' expressing confusion upon waking.
  • 曩樵者言钱某未至，吾来何速，今钱某往矣，吾其继之乎 - A complex sentence meaning 'The woodcutter said that Mr. Qian had not arrived, why did I come so quickly? Now that Mr. Qian has passed away, will I follow him?' This reflects a sense of premonition and fate.
  • 因记所梦，┑书箧中 - Means 'Xu then recorded his dream in a box,' indicating a personal record of the experience.
  • 及卒，家人发箧得之 - Means 'Upon his death, his family found it in the box,' suggesting a posthumous revelation.
  • 乃知石曼卿芙蓉之城，王平甫灵芝之宫，非小说家伪造也 - Means 'They then knew that the city of lotus flowers of Shi Manqing and the palace of lingzhi of Wang Pingfu were not fabricated by novelists,' confirming the authenticity of the visions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石曼卿 - Shi Manqing, a famous poet and scholar of the Song dynasty.
  • 芙蓉之城 - The city of lotus flowers, a metaphorical place associated with Shi Manqing.
  • 王平甫 - Wang Pingfu, another notable figure, possibly a contemporary or associate of Shi Manqing.
  • 灵芝之宫 - The palace of lingzhi, a mythical place associated with Wang Pingfu.
  • 小说家 - Refers to writers of novels, implying that these places were real and not fictional.

Proper Nouns

  • 徐 - Xu, the protagonist of the story.
  • 钱某 - Mr. Qian, a friend of Xu.
  • 石曼卿 - Shi Manqing, a historical figure.
  • 王平甫 - Wang Pingfu, another historical figure.
  • 芙蓉之城 - The city of lotus flowers, a metaphorical place.
  • 灵芝之宫 - The palace of lingzhi, a metaphorical place.
  • 小说家 - Writers of novels, a general term.

[833]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 素無賴 - Literally 'always without shame,' referring to a person who is morally corrupt or lazy.
  • 扌窟石 - A combination character that means 'to dig into rocks' or 'to quarry stones.'
  • 石欄石循 - Refers to stone railings and stone balustrades.
  • 載以小舟 - Means 'to load onto a small boat.'
  • 唐西鎮 - Refers to a specific town or market in the past.
  • 是夕忽得疾 - Means 'that night suddenly fell ill.'
  • 次日弥甚 - Means 'the next day even more severe.'
  • 具牲醴祷焉 - Means 'prepared sacrifices and wine to pray.'
  • 不数日，其子竟死 - Means 'within a few days, his son died.'
  • 乱后桥梁半就倾圮 - Means 'after chaos, half of the bridges have collapsed.'
  • 比来稍稍修葺 - Means 'recently, they have been repaired slightly.'
  • 甫修而即圮 - Means 'immediately after being repaired, they collapse.'
  • 推原其故 - Means 'to trace the cause.'
  • 盖有负之而趋者矣 - Means 'it is because someone carries it away.'
  • 安得处处有桥神 - Means 'how can there be bridge gods everywhere?'
  • 敛于兴隆桥边云 - Means 'he was buried near Xinglong Bridge.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杭州武林门内 - Refers to the area inside the Wulin Gate in Hangzhou, which is now part of modern-day Hangzhou.
  • 长寿桥 - Refers to a specific bridge named Changshou Bridge.
  • 唐西镇 - Refers to a market or town in the past.
  • 苏州山塘 - Refers to Shantang Street in Suzhou, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 兴隆桥 - Refers to Xinglong Bridge, another specific bridge.
  • 买棺至兴隆桥边敛我可也 - Refers to the practice of buying a coffin and burying oneself at a specific location.

Proper Nouns

  • 杭州 - The city of Hangzhou.
  • 武林门 - The Wulin Gate in Hangzhou.
  • 长寿桥 - Changshou Bridge.
  • 唐西镇 - A market or town in the past.
  • 苏州 - The city of Suzhou.
  • 山塘 - Shantang Street in Suzhou.
  • 兴隆桥 - Xinglong Bridge.

[834]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秀外惠中 - A phrase used to describe a woman who is both beautiful and intelligent.
  • 宿根者与 - A phrase suggesting a spiritual or karmic explanation for someone's fate.
  • 昙华一见 - A phrase meaning 'a fleeting encounter,' indicating a brief but significant meeting.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妾 - A concubine, a secondary wife in traditional Chinese society.
  • 奔丧 - Returning home for a funeral, a traditional practice in Chinese culture.
  • 寄养 - Entrusting a child to another family for care, a common practice in traditional Chinese society.
  • 殇 - The death of a young child, a term with specific cultural connotations.

Proper Nouns

  • 李翁 - A respectful title for an elderly man, likely a nickname or honorific.
  • 从化县 - A county in Guangdong Province, China.
  • 省城 - Refers to the provincial capital, likely Guangzhou.
  • 许仁山 - A scholar-official, likely a historical or fictional figure.
  • 海宁孙氏 - Refers to the Sun family of Hai Ning, a place in Zhejiang Province.
  • 小翠 - The name of Xu Renshan's daughter.
  • 京师 - Refers to the imperial capital, likely Beijing.

[835]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 出痘 - Classical term for smallpox, a disease that was widespread in ancient China.
  • 出花 - Another term for smallpox, often used in a more colloquial or poetic sense.
  • 上浆 - Refers to a stage in the development of smallpox where the pustules become firm and filled with fluid.
  • 易花 - Means to exchange flowers, but in this context, it symbolizes the exchange of fate or health.
  • 祸患常起于细微 - A proverb meaning that disasters often originate from small, seemingly insignificant events.
  • 焚如之祸 - A metaphor for a devastating fire, emphasizing the severity of the event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南北并有此语矣 - Indicates that the term '出花' is used in both the north and south of China, suggesting a widespread understanding of the disease.
  • 娣姒 - Refers to sisters-in-law, specifically the wife of a man's younger brother (娣) and the wife of a man's older brother (姒).
  • 卖花媪 - An elderly woman selling flowers, a common figure in traditional Chinese settings.
  • 火光荧荧 - Describes a faint, flickering light, often associated with supernatural or ominous signs.
  • 鼠衔灯草 - A common occurrence where a rat carries a piece of burning material, often leading to fires.
  • 祸患常起于细微 - A proverb attributed to Ouyang Xiu, a famous Song dynasty scholar, emphasizing the importance of paying attention to small details to prevent major problems.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈生端甫 - Teng Sheng Duanfu, a person mentioned in the second anecdote.
  • 扬州甘泉县 - A specific location in Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.
  • 仙女庙镇 - A town in Yangzhou, mentioned as the residence of Teng Sheng Duanfu.
  • 熊氏 - A surname, used to describe both the mother and the wife of Teng Sheng Duanfu.

[836]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少寡而贫 - Refers to a woman who is widowed at a young age and lives in poverty.
  • 惟为人缝纫以糊其口 - Means 'only by sewing and mending to make ends meet.'
  • 垂三十年矣 - Means 'for nearly thirty years.'
  • 颇饶于资 - Means 'quite wealthy.'
  • 有一子甫十岁 - Means 'has a son who is just ten years old.'
  • 自幼多病 - Means 'has been ill since childhood.'
  • 辄有鬼凭其身 - Means 'there is always a ghost possessing his body.'
  • 招巫者施敕勒之术 - Means 'calling upon a witch to perform exorcism rituals.'
  • 竟不能遣 - Means 'still cannot be driven away.'
  • 惟王媪偶过其家，则鬼即避之 - Means 'only when Wang Ao happens to pass by the house, the ghost immediately flees.'
  • 如是者数年 - Means 'like this for several years.'
  • 梦神语之曰 - Means 'he dreams that a god tells him.'
  • 以此子为王节妇后，病即愈矣 - Means 'if you let this son be the heir of Wang's chaste widow, his illness will heal.'
  • 愿以此子与媪共之，承两家香火 - Means 'wishes to give this son to Wang Ao to share, to carry on the ancestral rites of both families.'
  • 娶妻生子，奉王媪终其身 - Means 'marries and has children, serving Wang Ao until the end of her life.'
  • 殆鬼神哀怜节妇，不忍其老而无依与 - Means 'perhaps the spirits pity the chaste widow, and cannot bear to see her old and without support.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苏州 - Refers to Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 王媪 - Refers to Wang Ao, a woman in the story.
  • 节妇 - Refers to a chaste widow, a woman who remains loyal to her deceased husband.
  • 巫者 - Refers to a witch or shaman, someone who practices traditional healing and exorcism rituals.
  • 敕勒之术 - Refers to exorcism rituals performed by witches or shamans.
  • 香火 - Refers to the ancestral rites and sacrifices performed to honor ancestors.

Proper Nouns

  • 苏州 - Suzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 王媪 - Wang Ao, a woman in the story.
  • 节妇 - Chaste widow, a woman who remains loyal to her deceased husband.
  • 巫者 - Witch or shaman, someone who practices traditional healing and exorcism rituals.
  • 敕勒之术 - Exorcism rituals performed by witches or shamans.
  • 香火 - Ancestral rites and sacrifices performed to honor ancestors.

[837]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 許嫁 - Formal expression meaning 'to betroth' or 'to promise in marriage.'
  • 死生以之 - Phrase meaning 'to accept life and death with it,' indicating a commitment to the marriage regardless of outcome.
  • 適胡氏 - Means 'to marry into the Hu family,' where '適' is the formal verb for marriage.
  • 病有間 - Means 'to recover somewhat' or 'to improve temporarily,' where '間' indicates a brief respite.
  • 逾數年 - Means 'to pass several years,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 生二子 - Means 'to give birth to two sons,' where '生' is the verb for giving birth.
  • 始死 - Means 'to begin to die' or 'to pass away,' where '始' indicates the beginning of an action.
  • 撫其二子以終 - Means 'to care for her two sons until the end,' where '撫' means to nurture or care for.
  • 此與第十五卷所載樊氏女事相類 - Means 'This is similar to the story of Fan's daughter in the fifteenth volume,' where '相類' means 'similar' or 'analogous.'
  • 然樊女茕茕孤苦 - Means 'but Fan's daughter was lonely and suffering,' where '茕茕' emphasizes her isolation and '孤苦' means 'lonely and suffering.'
  • 張女則非止有節操，且有才干 - Means 'but Zhang's daughter not only had integrity but also talent,' where '非止' means 'not only' and '才干' means 'talent' or 'ability.'
  • 仕于闽为县令 - Means 'served in Fujian as a county magistrate,' where '仕' means 'to serve in office' and '县令' means 'county magistrate.'
  • 资财巨万 - Means 'possessed vast wealth,' where '巨万' is a hyperbolic expression for a very large amount.
  • 惟挟二妾以从 - Means 'only took two concubines with him,' where '惟' means 'only' and '挟' means 'to take along.'
  • 弃厥妻于家不顾 - Means 'abandoned his wife at home without concern,' where '弃' means 'to abandon' and '不顾' means 'without concern.'
  • 无以为生 - Means 'had no means of living,' where '无以' means 'no way to' or 'unable to.'
  • 纵臾其妻 - Means 'encouraged his wife,' where '纵臾' means 'to encourage' or 'to urge.'
  • 使如闽 - Means 'to send her to Fujian,' where '如' means 'to go to' or 'to travel to.'
  • 辞无资 - Means 'to decline due to lack of funds,' where '辞' means 'to decline' and '无资' means 'lack of funds.'
  • 为醵资 - Means 'to raise money,' where '醵资' means 'to collect money' or 'to raise funds.'
  • 又惧不礼于夫 - Means 'and feared that she would not be treated respectfully by her husband,' where '惧' means 'to fear' and '不礼' means 'not treated respectfully.'
  • 母无惧 - Means 'mother, do not fear,' where '无惧' means 'do not fear.'
  • 我侍母以行 - Means 'I will accompany you,' where '侍' means 'to accompany' or 'to serve.'
  • 然须具来往之资 - Means 'but we need to prepare the necessary funds,' where '具' means 'to prepare' and '来往之资' means 'funds for travel.'
  • 合则留，不合则返耳 - Means 'if we are welcomed, we will stay; if not, we will return,' where '合' means 'to welcome' and '返' means 'to return.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖北 - Refers to Hubei Province, located in central China.
  • 胡氏子 - Refers to the son of the Hu family.
  • 媒妁 - Refers to matchmakers or marriage brokers, who were important figures in traditional Chinese marriage arrangements.
  • 县令 - Refers to a county magistrate, a local government official responsible for administering a county.
  • 闽 - Refers to Fujian Province, located in southeastern China.
  • 资财巨万 - A hyperbolic expression indicating vast wealth, common in Classical Chinese to emphasize large amounts.
  • 妾 - Refers to a concubine, a secondary wife in a polygynous household.
  • 纵臾 - A term used to describe encouraging or urging someone, often with a negative connotation.
  • 醵资 - A term referring to the act of raising funds, often through a collective effort.

Proper Nouns

  • 湖北 - The name of a province in central China.
  • 胡氏 - The name of the Hu family.
  • 张氏 - The name of the Zhang family.
  • 闽 - The name of a province in southeastern China.
  • 县令 - The title of a county magistrate.
